moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure for capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation by de-risking the portfolio significantly.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 15-20% by selling positions.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest return of approximately 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces overall risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns.

moves.note: Add quality tech, strong scanner setup

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall leverage.
Sell SOXL completely and trim SPAL to 3% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it was flagged for trimming by the scanner.
Buy XLK at 10% and add to SMH at 15% to blend quality tech and semiconductors.
This plan balances growth with risk management, maintaining a small cash buffer.
The goal is to reduce concentration while investing in strong scanner setups.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for risk-first growth, targeting approximately 2.5% per month. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing new, quality tech exposure, leading to moderate expected drawdowns.

moves.note: Comply with mandate, free up capital

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate and free up capital for high-conviction plays.
Sell SOXL to exit the underperforming leveraged ETF, reallocating capital.
Trim QQQI to 10% to reduce income focus and increase growth-oriented positions.
Add to SMH at 20% and buy XLK at 15% to lean into strong semiconductor and tech sector setups.
Buy MRVL at 10% for its high risk/reward potential from the scanner.
This plan uses leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets higher growth, aiming for approximately 4% per month, by leaning into strong scanner setups and utilizing leverage. This approach significantly raises both upside potential and drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs can decay daily, requiring strict stop discipline to manage potential large losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free cash for income-focused investments.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing low drawdown.
Trim SMH and CRWD to 5% each to reduce high-beta single name and sector exposure.
Significantly add to QQQI, targeting 40% of the book, to prioritize monthly distributions and income.
This plan rotates the portfolio into income-generating ETFs and broad index for steady distributions.
Maintain a cash buffer to further reduce drawdown risk.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, focusing on steady monthly distributions and capital preservation. While prioritizing income, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff, meaning this is not a savings account but aims for low drawdown and reliable cash flow.
